text: This research explores the impact of the discussion method on student learning outcomes within the subject of Islamic Religious Education. Conducted at Madrasah Aliyah Negeri (MAN) 1 Tangerang, the study addresses the need for instructional strategies that foster critical thinking, self-confidence, and mutual respect among students. Utilizing a quantitative correlational approach, data were gathered from a sample of 38 eleventh-grade students through observations, Likert-scale questionnaires to measure the implementation of the discussion method (Variable X), and objective tests to assess learning outcomes (Variable Y). Statistical analysis using the Pearson Product Moment formula yielded a correlation coefficient ($r$) of 0.34, exceeding the critical table value of 0.32 at a 5% significance level. Furthermore, the $t$-test result ($t_{count} = 2.32 > t_{table} = 2.03$) confirms a significant positive influence. The findings indicate that the discussion method accounts for approximately 11% of the variance in student performance relative to materials on commendable and despicable morals. The study concludes that the discussion method is an effective pedagogical tool for enhancing student engagement and academic achievement in Islamic Education, providing a viable alternative to traditional teacher-centered approaches.
